title: CDK Configuration
description: JSON Schema for cdk.json. Generated from packages/aws-cdk/lib/cli/cli-config.ts. Do not edit by hand.

app.description: REQUIRED WHEN RUNNING APP: command-line for executing your app or a cloud assembly directory (e.g. "node bin/my-app.js"). Can also be specified in cdk.json or ~/.cdk.json
app.markdownDescription: REQUIRED WHEN RUNNING APP: command-line for executing your app or a cloud assembly directory (e.g. "node bin/my-app.js"). Can also be specified in cdk.json or ~/.cdk.json

assetMetadata.description: Include "aws:asset:*" CloudFormation metadata for resources that uses assets (enabled by default)
assetMetadata.markdownDescription: Include "aws:asset:*" CloudFormation metadata for resources that uses assets (enabled by default)

caBundlePath.description: Path to CA certificate to use when validating HTTPS requests. Will read from AWS_CA_BUNDLE environment variable if not specified
caBundlePath.markdownDescription: Path to CA certificate to use when validating HTTPS requests. Will read from AWS_CA_BUNDLE environment variable if not specified

ci.description: Force CI detection. If CI=true then logs will be sent to stdout instead of stderr
ci.markdownDescription: Force CI detection. If CI=true then logs will be sent to stdout instead of stderr

color.description: Force colored output even when stdout is not a TTY
color.markdownDescription: Force colored output even when stdout is not a TTY

debug.description: Debug the CDK app. Log additional information during synthesis, such as creation stack traces of tokens (sets CDK_DEBUG, will slow down synthesis)
debug.markdownDescription: Debug the CDK app. Log additional information during synthesis, such as creation stack traces of tokens (sets CDK_DEBUG, will slow down synthesis)

ec2creds.description: Force trying to fetch EC2 instance credentials. Default: guess EC2 instance status
ec2creds.markdownDescription: Force trying to fetch EC2 instance credentials. Default: guess EC2 instance status

ignoreErrors.description: Ignores synthesis errors, which will likely produce an invalid output
ignoreErrors.markdownDescription: Ignores synthesis errors, which will likely produce an invalid output

json.description: Use JSON output instead of YAML when templates are printed to STDOUT
json.markdownDescription: Use JSON output instead of YAML when templates are printed to STDOUT

lookups.description: Perform context lookups (synthesis fails if this is disabled and context lookups need to be performed)
lookups.markdownDescription: Perform context lookups (synthesis fails if this is disabled and context lookups need to be performed)

noColor.description: Removes colors and other style from console output
noColor.markdownDescription: Removes colors and other style from console output

notices.description: Configuration for `cdk notices`. Returns a list of relevant notices

unacknowledged.description: Returns a list of unacknowledged notices
unacknowledged.markdownDescription: Returns a list of unacknowledged notices

output.description: Emits the synthesized cloud assembly into a directory (default: cdk.out)
output.markdownDescription: Emits the synthesized cloud assembly into a directory (default: cdk.out)

pathMetadata.description: Include "aws:cdk:path" CloudFormation metadata for each resource (enabled by default)
pathMetadata.markdownDescription: Include "aws:cdk:path" CloudFormation metadata for each resource (enabled by default)

plugin.description: Name or path of a node package that extend the CDK features. Can be specified multiple times
plugin.markdownDescription: Name or path of a node package that extend the CDK features. Can be specified multiple times

profile.description: Use the indicated AWS profile as the default environment
profile.markdownDescription: Use the indicated AWS profile as the default environment

proxy.description: Use the indicated proxy. Will read from HTTPS_PROXY environment variable if not specified
proxy.markdownDescription: Use the indicated proxy. Will read from HTTPS_PROXY environment variable if not specified

region.description: Use the indicated AWS region as the default region
region.markdownDescription: Use the indicated AWS region as the default region

roleArn.description: ARN of Role to use when invoking CloudFormation
roleArn.markdownDescription: ARN of Role to use when invoking CloudFormation

staging.description: Copy assets to the output directory (use --no-staging to disable the copy of assets which allows local debugging via the SAM CLI to reference the original source files)
staging.markdownDescription: Copy assets to the output directory (use --no-staging to disable the copy of assets which allows local debugging via the SAM CLI to reference the original source files)

strict.description: Do not construct stacks with warnings
strict.markdownDescription: Do not construct stacks with warnings

telemetryFile.description: Send telemetry data to a local file.
telemetryFile.markdownDescription: Send telemetry data to a local file.

unstable.description: Opt in to unstable features. The flag indicates that the scope and API of a feature might still change. Otherwise the feature is generally production ready and fully supported. Can be specified multiple times.
unstable.markdownDescription: Opt in to unstable features. The flag indicates that the scope and API of a feature might still change. Otherwise the feature is generally production ready and fully supported. Can be specified multiple times.

verbose.description: Show debug logs (specify multiple times to increase verbosity)
verbose.markdownDescription: Show debug logs (specify multiple times to increase verbosity)

versionReporting.description: Disable CLI telemetry and do not include the "AWS::CDK::Metadata" resource in synthesized templates (enabled by default)
versionReporting.markdownDescription: Disable CLI telemetry and do not include the "AWS::CDK::Metadata" resource in synthesized templates (enabled by default)

yes.description: Automatically answer interactive prompts with the recommended response. This includes confirming actions.
yes.markdownDescription: Automatically answer interactive prompts with the recommended response. This includes confirming actions.

context.description: Context values for the CDK app. Keys are context keys, values are context values.

watch.description: Configuration for `cdk watch`. Includes file-watching patterns and deployment options.

include.description: File glob patterns to include in watch.

exclude.description: File glob patterns to exclude from watch.

buildExclude.description: Do not rebuild asset with the given ID. Can be specified multiple times
buildExclude.markdownDescription: Do not rebuild asset with the given ID. Can be specified multiple times

changeSetName.description: Name of the CloudFormation change set to create
changeSetName.markdownDescription: Name of the CloudFormation change set to create

concurrency.description: Maximum number of simultaneous deployments (dependency permitting) to execute.
concurrency.markdownDescription: Maximum number of simultaneous deployments (dependency permitting) to execute.

exclusively.description: Only deploy requested stacks, don't include dependencies
exclusively.markdownDescription: Only deploy requested stacks, don't include dependencies

force.description: Always deploy stack even if templates are identical
force.markdownDescription: Always deploy stack even if templates are identical

hotswap.description: Attempts to perform a 'hotswap' deployment, but does not fall back to a full deployment if that is not possible. Instead, changes to any non-hotswappable properties are ignored.'true' by default, use --no-hotswap to turn off
hotswap.markdownDescription: Attempts to perform a 'hotswap' deployment, but does not fall back to a full deployment if that is not possible. Instead, changes to any non-hotswappable properties are ignored.'true' by default, use --no-hotswap to turn off

hotswapEcsMaximumHealthyPercent.description: Upper limit on the number of your service's tasks that are allowed in the RUNNING or PENDING state during a deployment, as a percentage of the desiredCount
hotswapEcsMaximumHealthyPercent.markdownDescription: Upper limit on the number of your service's tasks that are allowed in the RUNNING or PENDING state during a deployment, as a percentage of the desiredCount

hotswapEcsMinimumHealthyPercent.description: Lower limit on the number of your service's tasks that must remain in the RUNNING state during a deployment, as a percentage of the desiredCount
hotswapEcsMinimumHealthyPercent.markdownDescription: Lower limit on the number of your service's tasks that must remain in the RUNNING state during a deployment, as a percentage of the desiredCount

hotswapEcsStabilizationTimeoutSeconds.description: Number of seconds to wait for a single service to reach stable state, where the desiredCount is equal to the runningCount
hotswapEcsStabilizationTimeoutSeconds.markdownDescription: Number of seconds to wait for a single service to reach stable state, where the desiredCount is equal to the runningCount

hotswapFallback.description: Attempts to perform a 'hotswap' deployment, which skips CloudFormation and updates the resources directly, and falls back to a full deployment if that is not possible.
hotswapFallback.markdownDescription: Attempts to perform a 'hotswap' deployment, which skips CloudFormation and updates the resources directly, and falls back to a full deployment if that is not possible.

logs.description: Show CloudWatch log events from all resources in the selected Stacks in the terminal. 'true' by default, use --no-logs to turn off
logs.markdownDescription: Show CloudWatch log events from all resources in the selected Stacks in the terminal. 'true' by default, use --no-logs to turn off

progress.description: Display mode for stack activity events
progress.markdownDescription: Display mode for stack activity events

rollback.description: Rollback stack to stable state on failure. Defaults to 'true', iterate more rapidly with --no-rollback or -R. Note: do **not** disable this flag for deployments with resource replacements, as that will always fail
rollback.markdownDescription: Rollback stack to stable state on failure. Defaults to 'true', iterate more rapidly with --no-rollback or -R. Note: do **not** disable this flag for deployments with resource replacements, as that will always fail

toolkitStackName.description: The name of the existing CDK toolkit stack (only used for app using legacy synthesis)
toolkitStackName.markdownDescription: The name of the existing CDK toolkit stack (only used for app using legacy synthesis)

list.description: Configuration for `cdk list`. Lists all stacks in the app

long.description: Display environment information for each stack
long.markdownDescription: Display environment information for each stack

showDependencies.description: Display stack dependency information for each stack
showDependencies.markdownDescription: Display stack dependency information for each stack

synth.description: Configuration for `cdk synth`. Synthesizes and prints the CloudFormation template for this stack

exclusively.description: Only synthesize requested stacks, don't include dependencies
exclusively.markdownDescription: Only synthesize requested stacks, don't include dependencies

quiet.description: Do not output CloudFormation Template to stdout
quiet.markdownDescription: Do not output CloudFormation Template to stdout

validation.description: After synthesis, validate stacks with the "validateOnSynth" attribute set (can also be controlled with CDK_VALIDATION)
validation.markdownDescription: After synthesis, validate stacks with the "validateOnSynth" attribute set (can also be controlled with CDK_VALIDATION)

bootstrap.description: Configuration for `cdk bootstrap`. Deploys the CDK toolkit stack into an AWS environment

bootstrapBucketName.description: The name of the CDK toolkit bucket; bucket will be created and must not exist
bootstrapBucketName.markdownDescription: The name of the CDK toolkit bucket; bucket will be created and must not exist

bootstrapCustomerKey.description: Create a Customer Master Key (CMK) for the bootstrap bucket (you will be charged but can customize permissions, modern bootstrapping only)
bootstrapCustomerKey.markdownDescription: Create a Customer Master Key (CMK) for the bootstrap bucket (you will be charged but can customize permissions, modern bootstrapping only)

bootstrapKmsKeyId.description: AWS KMS master key ID used for the SSE-KMS encryption (specify AWS_MANAGED_KEY to use an AWS-managed key)
bootstrapKmsKeyId.markdownDescription: AWS KMS master key ID used for the SSE-KMS encryption (specify AWS_MANAGED_KEY to use an AWS-managed key)

cloudformationExecutionPolicies.description: The Managed Policy ARNs that should be attached to the role performing deployments into this environment (may be repeated, modern bootstrapping only)
cloudformationExecutionPolicies.markdownDescription: The Managed Policy ARNs that should be attached to the role performing deployments into this environment (may be repeated, modern bootstrapping only)

customPermissionsBoundary.description: Use the permissions boundary specified by name.
customPermissionsBoundary.markdownDescription: Use the permissions boundary specified by name.

denyExternalId.description: Block AssumeRole access to all boostrapped roles if an ExternalId is provided (enabled by default)
denyExternalId.markdownDescription: Block AssumeRole access to all boostrapped roles if an ExternalId is provided (enabled by default)

execute.description: Whether to execute the change set (--no-execute will NOT execute the change set)
execute.markdownDescription: Whether to execute the change set (--no-execute will NOT execute the change set)

force.description: Always bootstrap even if it would downgrade template version
force.markdownDescription: Always bootstrap even if it would downgrade template version

importExistingResources.description: Whether to import existing resources into the bootstrap stack instead of failing if they already exist
importExistingResources.markdownDescription: Whether to import existing resources into the bootstrap stack instead of failing if they already exist

previousParameters.description: Use previous values for existing parameters (you must specify all parameters on every deployment if this is disabled)
previousParameters.markdownDescription: Use previous values for existing parameters (you must specify all parameters on every deployment if this is disabled)

publicAccessBlockConfiguration.description: Block public access configuration on CDK toolkit bucket (enabled by default)
publicAccessBlockConfiguration.markdownDescription: Block public access configuration on CDK toolkit bucket (enabled by default)

qualifier.description: String which must be unique for each bootstrap stack. You must configure it on your CDK app if you change this from the default.
qualifier.markdownDescription: String which must be unique for each bootstrap stack. You must configure it on your CDK app if you change this from the default.

showTemplate.description: Instead of actual bootstrapping, print the current CLI's bootstrapping template to stdout for customization
showTemplate.markdownDescription: Instead of actual bootstrapping, print the current CLI's bootstrapping template to stdout for customization

tags.description: Tags to add for the stack (KEY=VALUE)
tags.markdownDescription: Tags to add for the stack (KEY=VALUE)

template.description: Use the template from the given file instead of the built-in one (use --show-template to obtain an example)
template.markdownDescription: Use the template from the given file instead of the built-in one (use --show-template to obtain an example)

terminationProtection.description: Toggle CloudFormation termination protection on the bootstrap stacks
terminationProtection.markdownDescription: Toggle CloudFormation termination protection on the bootstrap stacks

toolkitStackName.description: The name of the CDK toolkit stack to create
toolkitStackName.markdownDescription: The name of the CDK toolkit stack to create

trust.description: The AWS account IDs that should be trusted to perform deployments into this environment (may be repeated, modern bootstrapping only)
trust.markdownDescription: The AWS account IDs that should be trusted to perform deployments into this environment (may be repeated, modern bootstrapping only)

trustForLookup.description: The AWS account IDs that should be trusted to look up values in this environment (may be repeated, modern bootstrapping only)
trustForLookup.markdownDescription: The AWS account IDs that should be trusted to look up values in this environment (may be repeated, modern bootstrapping only)

untrust.description: The AWS account IDs that should not be trusted by this environment (may be repeated, modern bootstrapping only)
untrust.markdownDescription: The AWS account IDs that should not be trusted by this environment (may be repeated, modern bootstrapping only)

gc.description: Configuration for `cdk gc`. Garbage collect assets. Options detailed here: https://github.com/aws/aws-cdk-cli/tree/main/packages/aws-cdk#cdk-gc

action.description: The action (or sub-action) you want to perform. Valid entires are "print", "tag", "delete-tagged", "full".
action.markdownDescription: The action (or sub-action) you want to perform. Valid entires are "print", "tag", "delete-tagged", "full".

bootstrapStackName.description: The name of the CDK toolkit stack, if different from the default "CDKToolkit" (deprecated, use --toolkit-stack-name). Deprecated: use --toolkit-stack-name
bootstrapStackName.markdownDescription: The name of the CDK toolkit stack, if different from the default "CDKToolkit" (deprecated, use --toolkit-stack-name). Deprecated: use --toolkit-stack-name

confirm.description: Confirm via manual prompt before deletion
confirm.markdownDescription: Confirm via manual prompt before deletion

createdBufferDays.description: Never delete assets younger than this (in days)
createdBufferDays.markdownDescription: Never delete assets younger than this (in days)

rollbackBufferDays.description: Delete assets that have been marked as isolated for this many days
rollbackBufferDays.markdownDescription: Delete assets that have been marked as isolated for this many days

toolkitStackName.description: The name of the CDK toolkit stack, if different from the default "CDKToolkit"
toolkitStackName.markdownDescription: The name of the CDK toolkit stack, if different from the default "CDKToolkit"

type.description: Specify either ecr, s3, or all
type.markdownDescription: Specify either ecr, s3, or all

flags.description: Configuration for `cdk flags`. View and toggle feature flags.

all.description: Modify or view all feature flags
all.markdownDescription: Modify or view all feature flags

concurrency.description: Maximum number of simultaneous synths to execute.
concurrency.markdownDescription: Maximum number of simultaneous synths to execute.

interactive.description: Interactive option for the flags command
interactive.markdownDescription: Interactive option for the flags command

safe.description: Enable all feature flags that do not impact the user's application
safe.markdownDescription: Enable all feature flags that do not impact the user's application

set.description: Signifies the user would like to modify their feature flag configuration
set.markdownDescription: Signifies the user would like to modify their feature flag configuration

value.description: The value the user would like to set the feature flag configuration to
value.markdownDescription: The value the user would like to set the feature flag configuration to

deploy.description: Configuration for `cdk deploy`. Deploys the stack(s) named STACKS into your AWS account

assetBuildConcurrency.description: Maximum number of asset builds to run in parallel
assetBuildConcurrency.markdownDescription: Maximum number of asset builds to run in parallel

assetParallelism.description: Whether to build/publish assets in parallel
assetParallelism.markdownDescription: Whether to build/publish assets in parallel

assetPrebuild.description: Whether to build all assets before deploying the first stack (useful for failing Docker builds)
assetPrebuild.markdownDescription: Whether to build all assets before deploying the first stack (useful for failing Docker builds)

buildExclude.description: Do not rebuild asset with the given ID. Can be specified multiple times
buildExclude.markdownDescription: Do not rebuild asset with the given ID. Can be specified multiple times

changeSetName.description: Name of the CloudFormation change set to create (only if method is not direct)
changeSetName.markdownDescription: Name of the CloudFormation change set to create (only if method is not direct)

concurrency.description: Maximum number of simultaneous deployments (dependency permitting) to execute.
concurrency.markdownDescription: Maximum number of simultaneous deployments (dependency permitting) to execute.

exclusively.description: Only deploy requested stacks, don't include dependencies
exclusively.markdownDescription: Only deploy requested stacks, don't include dependencies

execute.description: (Deprecated) Whether to execute the change set (--no-execute will NOT execute the change set) (deprecated)
execute.markdownDescription: (Deprecated) Whether to execute the change set (--no-execute will NOT execute the change set) (deprecated)

force.description: Always deploy stack even if templates are identical
force.markdownDescription: Always deploy stack even if templates are identical

hotswap.description: Attempts to perform a 'hotswap' deployment, but does not fall back to a full deployment if that is not possible. Instead, changes to any non-hotswappable properties are ignored.Do not use this in production environments
hotswap.markdownDescription: Attempts to perform a 'hotswap' deployment, but does not fall back to a full deployment if that is not possible. Instead, changes to any non-hotswappable properties are ignored.Do not use this in production environments

hotswapEcsMaximumHealthyPercent.description: Upper limit on the number of your service's tasks that are allowed in the RUNNING or PENDING state during a deployment, as a percentage of the desiredCount
hotswapEcsMaximumHealthyPercent.markdownDescription: Upper limit on the number of your service's tasks that are allowed in the RUNNING or PENDING state during a deployment, as a percentage of the desiredCount

hotswapEcsMinimumHealthyPercent.description: Lower limit on the number of your service's tasks that must remain in the RUNNING state during a deployment, as a percentage of the desiredCount
hotswapEcsMinimumHealthyPercent.markdownDescription: Lower limit on the number of your service's tasks that must remain in the RUNNING state during a deployment, as a percentage of the desiredCount

hotswapEcsStabilizationTimeoutSeconds.description: Number of seconds to wait for a single service to reach stable state, where the desiredCount is equal to the runningCount
hotswapEcsStabilizationTimeoutSeconds.markdownDescription: Number of seconds to wait for a single service to reach stable state, where the desiredCount is equal to the runningCount

hotswapFallback.description: Attempts to perform a 'hotswap' deployment, which skips CloudFormation and updates the resources directly, and falls back to a full deployment if that is not possible. Do not use this in production environments
hotswapFallback.markdownDescription: Attempts to perform a 'hotswap' deployment, which skips CloudFormation and updates the resources directly, and falls back to a full deployment if that is not possible. Do not use this in production environments

ignoreNoStacks.description: Whether to deploy if the app contains no stacks
ignoreNoStacks.markdownDescription: Whether to deploy if the app contains no stacks

importExistingResources.description: Indicates if the stack set imports resources that already exist.
importExistingResources.markdownDescription: Indicates if the stack set imports resources that already exist.

logs.description: Show CloudWatch log events from all resources in the selected Stacks in the terminal. 'true' by default, use --no-logs to turn off. Only in effect if specified alongside the '--watch' option
logs.markdownDescription: Show CloudWatch log events from all resources in the selected Stacks in the terminal. 'true' by default, use --no-logs to turn off. Only in effect if specified alongside the '--watch' option

method.description: How to perform the deployment. "change-set" (default) creates and executes a change set. "prepare-change-set" creates a change set without executing it. "execute-change-set" executes a previously created change set, bypassing synthesis entirely. "direct" skips change sets for faster deployments but lacks progress information
method.markdownDescription: How to perform the deployment. "change-set" (default) creates and executes a change set. "prepare-change-set" creates a change set without executing it. "execute-change-set" executes a previously created change set, bypassing synthesis entirely. "direct" skips change sets for faster deployments but lacks progress information

notificationArns.description: ARNs of SNS topics that CloudFormation will notify with stack related events. These will be added to ARNs specified with the 'notificationArns' stack property.
notificationArns.markdownDescription: ARNs of SNS topics that CloudFormation will notify with stack related events. These will be added to ARNs specified with the 'notificationArns' stack property.

outputsFile.description: Path to file where stack outputs will be written as JSON
outputsFile.markdownDescription: Path to file where stack outputs will be written as JSON

parameters.description: Additional parameters passed to CloudFormation at deploy time (STACK:KEY=VALUE)
parameters.markdownDescription: Additional parameters passed to CloudFormation at deploy time (STACK:KEY=VALUE)

previousParameters.description: Use previous values for existing parameters (you must specify all parameters on every deployment if this is disabled)
previousParameters.markdownDescription: Use previous values for existing parameters (you must specify all parameters on every deployment if this is disabled)

progress.description: Display mode for stack activity events
progress.markdownDescription: Display mode for stack activity events

revertDrift.description: Create a drift-aware change set that brings actual resource states in line with template definitions
revertDrift.markdownDescription: Create a drift-aware change set that brings actual resource states in line with template definitions

rollback.description: Rollback stack to stable state on failure. Defaults to 'true', iterate more rapidly with --no-rollback or -R. Note: do **not** disable this flag for deployments with resource replacements, as that will always fail
rollback.markdownDescription: Rollback stack to stable state on failure. Defaults to 'true', iterate more rapidly with --no-rollback or -R. Note: do **not** disable this flag for deployments with resource replacements, as that will always fail

tags.description: Tags to add to the stack (KEY=VALUE), overrides tags from Cloud Assembly (deprecated)
tags.markdownDescription: Tags to add to the stack (KEY=VALUE), overrides tags from Cloud Assembly (deprecated)

toolkitStackName.description: The name of the existing CDK toolkit stack (only used for app using legacy synthesis)
toolkitStackName.markdownDescription: The name of the existing CDK toolkit stack (only used for app using legacy synthesis)

watch.description: Continuously observe the project files, and deploy the given stack(s) automatically when changes are detected. Implies --hotswap by default
watch.markdownDescription: Continuously observe the project files, and deploy the given stack(s) automatically when changes are detected. Implies --hotswap by default

diagnose.description: Configuration for `cdk diagnose`. Find the root cause(s) of stack deployment failures

concurrency.description: How many stacks to diagnose in parallel
concurrency.markdownDescription: How many stacks to diagnose in parallel

toolkitStackName.description: The name of the existing CDK toolkit stack (only used for app using legacy synthesis)
toolkitStackName.markdownDescription: The name of the existing CDK toolkit stack (only used for app using legacy synthesis)

rollback.description: Configuration for `cdk rollback`. Rolls back the stack(s) named STACKS to their last stable state

force.description: Orphan all resources for which the rollback operation fails.
force.markdownDescription: Orphan all resources for which the rollback operation fails.

orphan.description: Orphan the given resources, identified by their logical ID (can be specified multiple times)
orphan.markdownDescription: Orphan the given resources, identified by their logical ID (can be specified multiple times)

toolkitStackName.description: The name of the CDK toolkit stack the environment is bootstrapped with
toolkitStackName.markdownDescription: The name of the CDK toolkit stack the environment is bootstrapped with

validateBootstrapVersion.description: Whether to validate the bootstrap stack version. Defaults to 'true', disable with --no-validate-bootstrap-version.
validateBootstrapVersion.markdownDescription: Whether to validate the bootstrap stack version. Defaults to 'true', disable with --no-validate-bootstrap-version.

publishAssets.description: Configuration for `cdk publish-assets`. Publish assets for the given stack(s) without deploying

all.description: Publish assets for all available stacks
all.markdownDescription: Publish assets for all available stacks

concurrency.description: Maximum number of simultaneous asset operations (building and publishing, dependency permitting) to execute.
concurrency.markdownDescription: Maximum number of simultaneous asset operations (building and publishing, dependency permitting) to execute.

exclusively.description: Only publish assets for requested stacks, don't include dependencies
exclusively.markdownDescription: Only publish assets for requested stacks, don't include dependencies

force.description: Always publish assets, even if they are already published
force.markdownDescription: Always publish assets, even if they are already published

import.description: Configuration for `cdk import`. Import existing resource(s) into the given STACK

changeSetName.description: Name of the CloudFormation change set to create
changeSetName.markdownDescription: Name of the CloudFormation change set to create

execute.description: Whether to execute the change set (--no-execute will NOT execute the change set)
execute.markdownDescription: Whether to execute the change set (--no-execute will NOT execute the change set)

force.description: Do not abort if the template diff includes updates or deletes. This is probably safe but we're not sure, let us know how it goes.
force.markdownDescription: Do not abort if the template diff includes updates or deletes. This is probably safe but we're not sure, let us know how it goes.

recordResourceMapping.description: If specified, CDK will generate a mapping of existing physical resources to CDK resources to be imported as. The mapping will be written in the given file path. No actual import operation will be performed
recordResourceMapping.markdownDescription: If specified, CDK will generate a mapping of existing physical resources to CDK resources to be imported as. The mapping will be written in the given file path. No actual import operation will be performed

resourceMapping.description: If specified, CDK will use the given file to map physical resources to CDK resources for import, instead of interactively asking the user. Can be run from scripts
resourceMapping.markdownDescription: If specified, CDK will use the given file to map physical resources to CDK resources for import, instead of interactively asking the user. Can be run from scripts

rollback.description: Rollback stack to stable state on failure. Defaults to 'true', iterate more rapidly with --no-rollback or -R. Note: do **not** disable this flag for deployments with resource replacements, as that will always fail
rollback.markdownDescription: Rollback stack to stable state on failure. Defaults to 'true', iterate more rapidly with --no-rollback or -R. Note: do **not** disable this flag for deployments with resource replacements, as that will always fail

toolkitStackName.description: The name of the CDK toolkit stack to create
toolkitStackName.markdownDescription: The name of the CDK toolkit stack to create

destroy.description: Configuration for `cdk destroy`. Destroy the stack(s) named STACKS

concurrency.description: Maximum number of simultaneous destroys (dependency permitting) to execute.
concurrency.markdownDescription: Maximum number of simultaneous destroys (dependency permitting) to execute.

exclusively.description: Only destroy requested stacks, don't include dependees
exclusively.markdownDescription: Only destroy requested stacks, don't include dependees

force.description: Do not ask for confirmation before destroying the stacks
force.markdownDescription: Do not ask for confirmation before destroying the stacks

diff.description: Configuration for `cdk diff`. Compares the specified stack with the deployed stack or a local template file, and returns with status 1 if any difference is found

changeSet.description: Whether to create a change set to analyze resource replacements. In this mode, diff will use the deploy role instead of the lookup role.. Deprecated: use --method instead
changeSet.markdownDescription: Whether to create a change set to analyze resource replacements. In this mode, diff will use the deploy role instead of the lookup role.. Deprecated: use --method instead

contextLines.description: Number of context lines to include in arbitrary JSON diff rendering
contextLines.markdownDescription: Number of context lines to include in arbitrary JSON diff rendering

exclusively.description: Only diff requested stacks, don't include dependencies
exclusively.markdownDescription: Only diff requested stacks, don't include dependencies

fail.description: Fail with exit code 1 in case of diff
fail.markdownDescription: Fail with exit code 1 in case of diff

importExistingResources.description: Whether or not the change set imports resources that already exist
importExistingResources.markdownDescription: Whether or not the change set imports resources that already exist

includeMoves.description: Whether to include moves in the diff
includeMoves.markdownDescription: Whether to include moves in the diff

method.description: How to compute the diff. "auto" attempts to create a change set and falls back to template-only on failure. "change-set" creates a change set and fails if it cannot be created. Both use the deploy role instead of the lookup role. "template" compares templates directly and uses the lookup role.
method.markdownDescription: How to compute the diff. "auto" attempts to create a change set and falls back to template-only on failure. "change-set" creates a change set and fails if it cannot be created. Both use the deploy role instead of the lookup role. "template" compares templates directly and uses the lookup role.

processed.description: Whether to compare against the template with Transforms already processed
processed.markdownDescription: Whether to compare against the template with Transforms already processed

quiet.description: Do not print stack name and default message when there is no diff to stdout
quiet.markdownDescription: Do not print stack name and default message when there is no diff to stdout

securityOnly.description: Only diff for broadened security changes
securityOnly.markdownDescription: Only diff for broadened security changes

strict.description: Do not filter out AWS::CDK::Metadata resources, mangled non-ASCII characters, or the CheckBootstrapVersionRule
strict.markdownDescription: Do not filter out AWS::CDK::Metadata resources, mangled non-ASCII characters, or the CheckBootstrapVersionRule

template.description: The path to the CloudFormation template to compare with. Implies --method=template
template.markdownDescription: The path to the CloudFormation template to compare with. Implies --method=template

drift.description: Configuration for `cdk drift`. Detect drifts in the given CloudFormation stack(s)

fail.description: Fail with exit code 1 if drift is detected
fail.markdownDescription: Fail with exit code 1 if drift is detected

init.description: Configuration for `cdk init`. Create a new, empty CDK project from a template.

fromPath.description: Path to a local custom template directory or multi-template repository
fromPath.markdownDescription: Path to a local custom template directory or multi-template repository

generateOnly.description: If true, only generates project files, without executing additional operations such as setting up a git repo, installing dependencies or compiling the project
generateOnly.markdownDescription: If true, only generates project files, without executing additional operations such as setting up a git repo, installing dependencies or compiling the project

language.description: The language to be used for the new project (default can be configured in ~/.cdk.json)
language.markdownDescription: The language to be used for the new project (default can be configured in ~/.cdk.json)

libVersion.description: The version of the CDK library (aws-cdk-lib) to initialize built-in templates with. Defaults to the version that was current when this CLI was built.
libVersion.markdownDescription: The version of the CDK library (aws-cdk-lib) to initialize built-in templates with. Defaults to the version that was current when this CLI was built.

packageManager.description: The package manager to use to install dependencies. Only applicable for TypeScript and JavaScript projects. Defaults to npm in TypeScript and JavaScript projects.
packageManager.markdownDescription: The package manager to use to install dependencies. Only applicable for TypeScript and JavaScript projects. Defaults to npm in TypeScript and JavaScript projects.

projectName.description: The name of the new project
projectName.markdownDescription: The name of the new project

templatePath.description: Path to a specific template within a multi-template repository
templatePath.markdownDescription: Path to a specific template within a multi-template repository

migrate.description: Configuration for `cdk migrate`. Migrate existing AWS resources into a CDK app

account.description: The account to retrieve the CloudFormation stack template from
account.markdownDescription: The account to retrieve the CloudFormation stack template from

compress.description: Use this flag to zip the generated CDK app
compress.markdownDescription: Use this flag to zip the generated CDK app

filter.description:
Filters the resource scan based on the provided criteria in the following format: "key1=value1,key2=value2"
 This field can be passed multiple times for OR style filtering: 
 filtering options: 
 resource-identifier: A key-value pair that identifies the target resource. i.e. {"ClusterName", "myCluster"}
 resource-type-prefix: A string that represents a type-name prefix. i.e. "AWS::DynamoDB::"
 tag-key: a string that matches resources with at least one tag with the provided key. i.e. "myTagKey"
 tag-value: a string that matches resources with at least one tag with the provided value. i.e. "myTagValue"
filter.markdownDescription:
Filters the resource scan based on the provided criteria in the following format: "key1=value1,key2=value2"
 This field can be passed multiple times for OR style filtering: 
 filtering options: 
 resource-identifier: A key-value pair that identifies the target resource. i.e. {"ClusterName", "myCluster"}
 resource-type-prefix: A string that represents a type-name prefix. i.e. "AWS::DynamoDB::"
 tag-key: a string that matches resources with at least one tag with the provided key. i.e. "myTagKey"
 tag-value: a string that matches resources with at least one tag with the provided value. i.e. "myTagValue"

fromPath.description: The path to the CloudFormation template to migrate. Use this for locally stored templates
fromPath.markdownDescription: The path to the CloudFormation template to migrate. Use this for locally stored templates

fromScan.description:
Determines if a new scan should be created, or the last successful existing scan should be used 
 options are "new" or "most-recent"
fromScan.markdownDescription:
Determines if a new scan should be created, or the last successful existing scan should be used 
 options are "new" or "most-recent"

fromStack.description: Use this flag to retrieve the template for an existing CloudFormation stack
fromStack.markdownDescription: Use this flag to retrieve the template for an existing CloudFormation stack

language.description: The language to be used for the new project
language.markdownDescription: The language to be used for the new project

outputPath.description: The output path for the migrated CDK app
outputPath.markdownDescription: The output path for the migrated CDK app

region.description: The region to retrieve the CloudFormation stack template from
region.markdownDescription: The region to retrieve the CloudFormation stack template from

stackName.description: The name assigned to the stack created in the new project. The name of the app will be based off this name as well.
stackName.markdownDescription: The name assigned to the stack created in the new project. The name of the app will be based off this name as well.

docs.description: Configuration for `cdk docs`. Opens the reference documentation in a browser

browser.description: the command to use to open the browser, using %u as a placeholder for the path of the file to open
browser.markdownDescription: the command to use to open the browser, using %u as a placeholder for the path of the file to open

refactor.description: Configuration for `cdk refactor`. Moves resources between stacks or within the same stack

additionalStackName.description: Names of deployed stacks to be considered for resource comparison.
additionalStackName.markdownDescription: Names of deployed stacks to be considered for resource comparison.

dryRun.description: Do not perform any changes, just show what would be done
dryRun.markdownDescription: Do not perform any changes, just show what would be done

force.description: Whether to do the refactor without asking for confirmation
force.markdownDescription: Whether to do the refactor without asking for confirmation

overrideFile.description: A file that declares overrides to be applied to the list of mappings computed by the CLI.
overrideFile.markdownDescription: A file that declares overrides to be applied to the list of mappings computed by the CLI.

revert.description: If specified, the command will revert the refactor operation. This is only valid if a mapping file was provided.
revert.markdownDescription: If specified, the command will revert the refactor operation. This is only valid if a mapping file was provided.

cliTelemetry.description: Configuration for `cdk cli-telemetry`. Enable or disable anonymous telemetry
